key_concerns: Early overall survival detriment observed in the cilta-cel arm
Higher rate of deaths due to adverse events in the cilta-cel arm (11% vs 8%)
Significant underreporting of Grade 3-4 lymphopenia not recovered by Day 30 (corrected from 3.2% to 29.3%)
Neurological toxicities including encephalopathy and parkinsonism
Retrospective identification of high-risk subpopulations suggests post-hoc data exploration
Increased rate of early deaths before disease progression in the cilta-cel arm (8% vs 2%)
key_positives: Median duration of response has not been reached, indicating durable responses
Corrections are minor in some areas and do not fundamentally alter the efficacy claims
Serious adverse event rates similar between arms after correction (37.8% vs 38.9%)

evidence_notes: Corrected SAE rates slightly higher but similar between arms (37.8% vs 38.9%) [Errata item 1: 'Serious adverse events (SAEs) occurred in 37.8% and 38.9% of patients in the cilta-cel and standard therapy arms, respectively.']
Duration of response not reached, suggesting ongoing benefit [Errata item 2: 'median duration of response has not been reached.']
Early OS detriment acknowledged, with more deaths due to AEs in cilta-cel arm [Errata item 17: 'more deaths due to AEs occurred in the cilta-cel arm (11%) compared to the standard therapy arm (8%)']
Significant correction of lymphopenia grade 3-4 not recovered by Day 30 from 3.2% to 29.3% [Errata item 16: 'row 2 (lymphopenia) ... reads 6 (3.2%) should read 55 (29.3%).']

Higher proportion of early deaths before disease progression in cilta-cel arm [Errata item 17: 'higher proportion of subjects died before disease progression in the cilta-cel arm (8%) compared to the standard therapy arm (2%)']

Five high-risk subpopulations retrospectively identified, including rapid progressors, extramedullary disease, high beta-2 microglobulin, poor ECOG, and early progressors on cilta-cel [Errata item 19: 'These five high-risk subpopulations were retrospectively identified...']
key_concerns: Early overall survival detriment observed in the cilta-cel arm
Higher rate of deaths due to adverse events in the cilta-cel arm (11% vs 8%)
Significant underreporting of Grade 3-4 lymphopenia not recovered by Day 30 (corrected from 3.2% to 29.3%)
Neurological toxicities including encephalopathy and parkinsonism
Retrospective identification of high-risk subpopulations suggests post-hoc data exploration
Increased rate of early deaths before disease progression in the cilta-cel arm (8% vs 2%)
key_positives: Median duration of response has not been reached, indicating durable responses
Corrections are minor in some areas and do not fundamentally alter the efficacy claims
Serious adverse event rates similar between arms after correction (37.8% vs 38.9%)

questions_framing.description: FDA questions are expected to focus on the observed early overall survival detriment and increased toxicity in the cilta-cel arm, seeking committee input on the benefit-risk assessment for the proposed earlier-line indication.

document.title: March 15, 2024 Meeting of the Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee- FDA Briefing Document- Errata (CARVYKTI)

signal.label: MIXED
rationale: Concerns: Early overall survival detriment observed in the cilta-cel arm; Higher rate of deaths due to adverse events in the cilta-cel arm (11% vs 8%); Significant underreporting of Grade 3-4 lymphopenia not recovered by Day 30 (corrected from 3.2% to 29.3%); Neurological toxicities including encephalopathy and parkinsonism; Retrospective identification of high-risk subpopulations suggests post-hoc data exploration

Positives: Median duration of response has not been reached, indicating durable responses; Corrections are minor in some areas and do not fundamentally alter the efficacy claims; Serious adverse event rates similar between arms after correction (37.8% vs 38.9%)
